$policy: See docs/design/execution/chunk-0a/anchors.md §3.3 and docs/design/execution/chunk-0a/brief.md §3.3 (R12 / plan §15 rule 5). Every file under tests/fixtures/goldens/** (excluding this registry itself and fixture-refs.ts modules) must have exactly one entry below. designation is one of: "frozen-migration-input" (the asset is a preservation oracle — a chunk that touches its surface must reproduce it byte-for-byte/outcome-for-outcome; never re-recorded outside its owning chunk) or "re-baseline" (the asset deliberately baselines behavior a later chunk is going to change on purpose; reBaselineChunk names the manifest chunk id — see docs/design/akm-0.9.0-chunk-manifest.json — where it is reviewed and re-captured). reBaselineChunk is required iff designation is "re-baseline" and must be absent otherwise. consumers[] lists the suite(s) that load the fixture via tests/_helpers/golden.ts's expectGolden/loadGolden. Policed by tests/goldens-designations.test.ts. | Amendment (2026-07-16 gate hardening): (1) every frozen-migration-input entry carries a sha256 of its asset bytes, verified by scripts/lint-goldens-presence.ts on every lint run — AKM_UPDATE_GOLDENS=1 cannot silently mutate a frozen oracle; a legitimate re-baseline updates designation+reBaselineChunk+sha256-removal in the same reviewed change. (2) Surface-owner rule: a chunk that the plan orders to change an asset's pinned surface becomes that asset's re-baseline owner — re-designate the entry (designation, reBaselineChunk, notes) BEFORE the surface change lands, so the re-capture is a reviewed diff, not a frozen-asset violation.

entries.notes: WI-03, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R3 (engines 1+2 of 3: proposal accept/revert + reject). Preservation oracle through Chunk 6's collapse into one FileChange transaction. Pins accept (new-asset, overwrite-with-backup, idempotent re-accept short-circuit, target-mutated-during-displace abort), revert (success restore, refuse-clobber), reject (success, non-pending UsageError, concurrent-edit succeeds -- reject has NO before-hash), and createProposal dedup/cooldown/force skip-record shapes, as file-tree/DB-status/exactly-once-event outcomes only -- no journal bytes or paths (brief §3.2 rule 4). Contains fixture-local names only (tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/fixture-refs.ts) — no production refs. Caveat: the `fileTree` object's keys are relative asset paths (e.g. `lessons/<fixture-local-name>.md`) that embed the same fixture-local name a ref would carry; if Chunk 5's §15.2 ref-grammar codemod changes how names map to on-disk paths, these path keys (not the outcome booleans/counts) would need re-baselining alongside that migration even though this asset is otherwise designated frozen. Also records a captured DEVIATION from the brief's testsFirst description for the target-mutated-during-displace scenario (see the suite's file-header comment): the real surfaced abort error is 'Cannot roll back proposal transaction: <path> diverged.', not 'Proposal target changed while its backup was being acquired.', and the target is left holding the mutator's content rather than being byte-identically restored.

WI-03, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R3 (crash-recovery half of engines 1+2 of 3). Integration scope (brief §3.4) — parameterizes the existing, unmodified tests/integration/_helpers/proposal-crash-runner.ts subprocess harness. Pins SIGKILL recovery outcomes for accept (all 5 pre-commit phases: prepared rolls back, every later phase rolls forward), revert (exactly-once per phase), reject (exactly-one rejected event per phase incl. the prepared/state-persisted/event-finalized set), and the reject-recovers-a-pending-accept ordering (akmProposalReject recovers an interrupted accept transaction BEFORE evaluating its own pending-status guard). journalPhasesObserved is informational only (brief §3.2 rule 4) — the single phase name the runner was told to hold at, never journal bytes/paths. Same fileTree-key caveat as tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/proposal-txn.json does not apply here (this fixture serializes only booleans/status strings/event counts, no fileTree). | Re-designated 2026-07-16 (gate hardening, false-positive audit): this asset pins the OLD journal engine's observable shape (phase vocabulary / journal location / journal write counts), and Chunk 6 is ordered by the plan to replace that engine with one FileChange transaction — Chunk 6 therefore OWNS this asset's re-capture (reviewed diff + ledger entry in the same chunk). The outcome-level txn oracles (proposal-txn.json, move-txn.json) stay frozen: they pin file-tree/DB-status/event outcomes designed to survive the engine swap.

WI-04, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R3 (engine 3 of 3: mv move engine). Preservation oracle through Chunk 6's collapse into one FileChange transaction. Pins the move-with-citers scenario (body-ref + frontmatter-ref + task-yaml citers + .derived.md twin: fileTree, rewrote counts, readOnlyCiters, exactly-one mv event), the divergent-citer abort at both the STAGE (mv-cli.ts:576) and REPLACE (:632) windows, the divergent-committed-target recovery refusal (validateCommittedMove :453-474, roll-forward-only per :1394-1395), and the transient index re-key failure retaining the journal until a later mutation completes it forward -- all as file-tree/citer-rewrite/exactly-once-event outcomes only, no journal bytes or paths (brief §3.2 rule 4). Contains fixture-local names only (tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/fixture-refs.ts) — no production refs. Caveat (same class as tests/fixtures/goldens/journal/proposal-txn.json): the `moveWithCiters.fileTree`/`rewroteRelPaths`/`rewroteCounts` keys are relative asset paths embedding fixture-local names, and `moveWithCiters.from`/`to` are literal fixture-local ref strings; if Chunk 5's §15.2 ref-grammar codemod changes how names map to on-disk paths or ref spellings, these path/ref fields (not the outcome booleans/counts) would need re-baselining alongside that migration even though this asset is otherwise designated frozen. Also records a captured DEVIATION from the brief's testsFirst description (see the suite's file-header comment): `_setMvMutationHookForTests` never fires between the stage and replace windows (only post-filesystem-commit), so spyOn interception is used instead; and the REPLACE window's real surfaced error is a wrapped 'Move failed (refusing to replace divergent citer <path>) and rollback failed (cannot restore <path>: file diverged after exclusive ownership).' string, not a bare prefix.

WI-04, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R3 (crash-recovery half of engine 3 of 3). Integration scope (brief §3.4) — parameterizes the existing, unmodified tests/integration/_helpers/mv-crash-runner.ts subprocess harness. Pins SIGKILL recovery outcomes: 'applying' rolls back in full (byte-identical tree, before any citer/source rename); filesystem-committed/index-finalized/state-finalized/event-finalized each roll forward via finalizeMoveTransaction to the same end state with exactly-one mv event and re-keyed index + state rows; and the FOUR independent recovery entry points (mv run's own pre-flight recovery mv-cli.ts:1237, proposal promotion repository.ts:1702, the full indexer indexer.ts:558, the targeted write-path indexer index-written-assets.ts:72) each independently finish the SAME kind of interrupted mv journal, pinning that the mv journal lives IN-STASH (<stashDir>/.akm/mv-transactions) unlike the proposal journals which live in getDataDir(). journalPhasesObserved is informational only (brief §3.2 rule 4). No ref-grammar caveat: this fixture serializes only booleans/counts/phase-name strings, no fileTree or ref literals. | Re-designated 2026-07-16 (gate hardening, false-positive audit): this asset pins the OLD journal engine's observable shape (phase vocabulary / journal location / journal write counts), and Chunk 6 is ordered by the plan to replace that engine with one FileChange transaction — Chunk 6 therefore OWNS this asset's re-capture (reviewed diff + ledger entry in the same chunk). The outcome-level txn oracles (proposal-txn.json, move-txn.json) stay frozen: they pin file-tree/DB-status/event outcomes designed to survive the engine swap.

WI-05, plan §15.7 / R5. Chunk 7 DoD 5 oracle for the four consolidate op-handlers (handleMergeOp/handleDeleteOp/handlePromoteOp/handleContradictOp, consolidate.ts:2117/2416/2477/2693) extracted out of akmConsolidateInner's op-execution loop — must stay green through Chunk 7's decomposition of that loop. Pins merge (1+1 primary byte-for-byte after <TS> norm + secondary archived-with-superseded_by-then-deleted; 1+2 secondaries' deliberate one-generateMergedContent-call/all-archived asymmetry, captured via the REAL generateMergedContent with _setChatCompletionForTests stubbed since the asymmetry is a property of that function's secondaries[0]-only read; the refusal matrix for hot/unparseable participants and missing/truncated description and the anti-collapse generation guard), delete (normal/hot-refused/already-gone), promote (happy path + the six named gates in consolidate.ts:2477-2690 order), and contradict (>=0.92 byte-for-byte timestamp-free, idempotent re-run, archived state preserved, <0.92 skip, missing confidence defaults to 1.0). Contains fixture-local names only (tests/fixtures/goldens/consolidate/fixture-refs.ts) — no production refs. Archive filenames embed a timestampForFilename() token and are recorded as array VALUES (never object keys) so <TS> normalization applies. DEVIATION from the brief's step 2 prose (see the suite's file-header comment): most scenarios inject a generateMergedContentFn directly into ConsolidateOpContext and drive the handlers directly (mirroring tests/commands/consolidate/consolidate-op-handlers.test.ts) rather than stubbing chunk-plan selection via akmConsolidate + _setChatCompletionForTests — WI-05 scopes to op OUTCOMES, not op SELECTION (planConsolidation's concern, covered by consolidate-chunks.test.ts and WI-06). The 'hot memories via remember hot-capture path' step is satisfied by writing captureMode:hot/beliefState:asserted frontmatter directly (matching remember-cli.ts:188,288's own output shape) rather than invoking the rememberCommand CLI entry point, consistent with how all 13 existing consolidate suites mint hot fixtures.

WI-05, plan §15.7 / R5. Pure-function preservation oracle for mergePlans (consolidate/merge.ts:35, precedence rules :80-110): hallucinated-ref drop (primary and secondary), merge-wins-over-delete (both chunk orders), promote-queued-before-merge (ops ARRAY ORDER, since applyConsolidationPlan processes ops in array order), and contradict pair-dedup by ref|contradictedByRef (keeping the first occurrence). Byte-for-byte JSON in/out — mergePlans has no fs/LLM/config/timestamp/id dependency, so no <TS>/<ID> placeholders apply; this is the one WI-05 asset with zero normalization surface. Contains fixture-local names only. Extends (does not duplicate) tests/commands/consolidate/consolidate-promote-dedup.test.ts's mergePlans promote-by-ref dedup coverage.

WI-06, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R5. Preservation oracle for the consolidate journal's full-run write/complete/cleanup lifecycle (getJournalPath/writeJournal :737-747/markJournalCompleted :749-759/cleanupJournal :761-774/backupFile :776-783, all module-private — observed via spyOn(fs.writeFileSync/copyFileSync) around a REAL akmConsolidate run, same src/-change-free technique as WI-04's goldens-mv-txn.test.ts) and the all-hot chunk early-exit (:1617-1636: zero LLM calls, every memory bucketed judgedNoAction). Journal write shape/count is informational data only (brief §3.2 rule 4) — never asserted against raw journal bytes or directory layout, only observable outcomes (write count, parsed completed/operations arrays, end-state file/dir existence). Caveat (same class as the WI-03/04 fileTree-key caveats): fullRunLifecycle.firstJournalWrite/lastJournalWrite.operations[].ref embeds a fixture-local ref (tests/fixtures/goldens/consolidate/fixture-refs.ts) that would need re-keying alongside Chunk 5's §15.2 grammar codemod even though this asset is otherwise designated frozen; the outcome booleans/counts are not affected. No production refs anywhere in the fixture. | Re-designated 2026-07-16 (gate hardening, false-positive audit): this asset pins the OLD journal engine's observable shape (phase vocabulary / journal location / journal write counts), and Chunk 6 is ordered by the plan to replace that engine with one FileChange transaction — Chunk 6 therefore OWNS this asset's re-capture (reviewed diff + ledger entry in the same chunk). The outcome-level txn oracles (proposal-txn.json, move-txn.json) stay frozen: they pin file-tree/DB-status/event outcomes designed to survive the engine swap.

WI-06, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R5. Preservation oracle for checkForIncompleteJournal's recovery-mode matrix (:692-735, invoked at akmConsolidate entry :1012): incomplete journal + recoveryMode 'abort' (default) throws ConfigError INVALID_CONFIG_FILE with a backup-dir hint and removes nothing; an unreadable (malformed JSON) journal throws the same code under abort; recoveryMode 'clean' on an incomplete journal calls removeStaleJournal (:665-690) and removes both the journal and its backup dir, for both the explicit backupTimestamp field and the startedAt-derived ([:.]->-) fallback; and a 'completed >= operations' journal is silently treated as NOT incomplete (no throw, no removal) — CHARACTERIZATION WARNING (plan §15.5, brief Risk 8): this is a genuinely surprising, previously-uncovered outcome — such a journal's own backup directory becomes a permanent orphan that no code path this suite can find ever reclaims; captured as-is, not fixed. No ref literals appear in this fixture (booleans, counts, and the ConfigError code string only) — no ref-grammar re-baseline caveat applies. | Re-designated 2026-07-16 (gate hardening, false-positive audit): this asset pins the OLD journal engine's observable shape (phase vocabulary / journal location / journal write counts), and Chunk 6 is ordered by the plan to replace that engine with one FileChange transaction — Chunk 6 therefore OWNS this asset's re-capture (reviewed diff + ledger entry in the same chunk). The outcome-level txn oracles (proposal-txn.json, move-txn.json) stay frozen: they pin file-tree/DB-status/event outcomes designed to survive the engine swap.

WI-06, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R5. Preservation oracle for consolidateGuardStatus's four-way verdict (consolidate/eligibility.ts:60): hot (captureMode:hot frontmatter), safe (ordinary frontmatter), unparseable (zero-key/no-frontmatter body), and missing (file absent). Prompt-level hot-list annotation is a different concern already covered by tests/commands/consolidate/consolidate-chunks.test.ts — referenced, not duplicated here. No ref literals in this fixture (verdict strings only). | Re-designated 2026-07-16 (gate hardening, false-positive audit): this asset pins the OLD journal engine's observable shape (phase vocabulary / journal location / journal write counts), and Chunk 6 is ordered by the plan to replace that engine with one FileChange transaction — Chunk 6 therefore OWNS this asset's re-capture (reviewed diff + ledger entry in the same chunk). The outcome-level txn oracles (proposal-txn.json, move-txn.json) stay frozen: they pin file-tree/DB-status/event outcomes designed to survive the engine swap.

WI-06, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R5, §6 preserve list. The signal-delta gate SURVIVES Chunk 7's deletion of the P0-A high-retrieval lane and self-consistency voting (both pinned separately as re-baseline @ 7 by improve/self-consistency.json and improve/p0a-selection.json). Pins isSignalDeltaEligible's truth table (eligibility.ts:421-431, pure function), and the eligibleRefs/distillOnlyRefs/noFeedbackPool partition COUNTS from one full akmImprove preparation run with proactiveMaintenance disabled and zero retrievals/salience seeded so only the signal-delta gate can ever select a ref — P0-A lane membership and SC voting internals are deliberately out of scope (brief Risk 9); only the shared 'signal-delta' eligibilitySource tag (the gate's own vocabulary, not a P0-A-specific algorithm) is asserted. buildLatestFeedbackTsMap's signal/note filter and buildLatestProposalTsMap's reflect/distill cursor rules are exercised directly by the suite's assertion tests (state.db-backed) but are not separately serialized into this fixture beyond the truth-table/partition-count sections. Contains only fixture-local ref names (tests/fixtures/goldens/improve/fixture-refs.ts), booleans, and counts — no production refs.

WI-07, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R4 (family E non-CLI residues). Pins parseSinceToIso's (consolidate.ts:2824, module-private) identity-fallback behavior on unparseable --since input, exercised indirectly through the exported narrowToIncrementalCandidates: garbage input never selects any memory (lexicographic string-comparison artifact of the identity fallback), a valid duration/ISO input does. Config-driven only (incrementalSince) — not reachable from any CLI flag. Contains only fixture-local names (tests/fixtures/goldens/cli/fixture-refs.ts) and a normalized <TS> placeholder — no production refs.

WI-07, plan §11 Chunk 0a / R4 (family E non-CLI residues). Pins resolveRelativeDates' (memory-improve.ts:362-391, module-private) phrase grammar (yesterday / last week|month|year / N days|weeks|months ago) against a pinned frontmatter createdAt referenceDate, exercised indirectly through the exported applyMemoryCleanup. Memory-CONTENT phrase rewriting, NOT flag parsing — captured so it is not silently folded into the duration-flag grammar. Contains only fixture-local names (tests/fixtures/goldens/cli/fixture-refs.ts) and a normalized <TS> placeholder — no production refs.
